hero.description: Join ENSET Challenge 2026 - Morocco's premier student competition for AI innovation and cybersecurity excellence, taking place during the Global Entrepreneurship and Innovation Week.

keyPoint.label: Elite Networking
keyPoint.value: Connect with industry leaders, academic experts, and Morocco's top student innovators.

keyPoint.label: Agentic AI Impact Hackathon
keyPoint.value: A 36-hour sprint to architect autonomous AI agents that drive progress across educational and industrial ecosystems.

keyPoint.label: Intense Cybersecurity CTF
keyPoint.value: 24-hour Capture The Flag competition testing offensive and defensive cybersecurity skills.

keyPoint.label: Expert Workshops & Mentorship
keyPoint.value: Receive guidance from professionals and academics in targeted workshops and personalized mentorship sessions.

about.title: ENSET CHALLENGE 2026
about.description: As a highlight of the Global Entrepreneurship and Innovation Week (GEIW), ENSET Challenge 2026 is meticulously organized by the student tech clubs of ENSET Mohammedia (N7Geeks, GDG On Campus, N7SEC). Our mission is to cultivate a dynamic ecosystem for technological innovation and cybersecurity excellence among Morocco's future leaders.
highlight: A 36-hour sprint to architect autonomous AI agents that drive progress across educational and industrial ecosystems.
Rigorous online qualifiers culminating in a high-stakes 24-hour final cybersecurity challenge.
Access exclusive workshops and benefit from direct mentorship by industry and academic experts.
Connect with leading companies, influential professionals, and talented peers from across Morocco's tech ecosystem.

competition.title: The ENSET Challenge 2026 Arenas
competition.subdescription: Showcase your mastery in Artificial Intelligence or Cybersecurity.
competition.description: Choose your battleground. Participate in our flagship competitions to innovate, learn, network, and contend for prestigious awards and career-defining internship opportunities.

hackathon.title: ENSET Challenge Hackathon: Agentic AI for Progress
hackathon.description: Engage in a 36-hour marathon to build autonomous AI agents that drive real-world progress in education and industry. Build smart, interactive ecosystems and deploy tools with real-world impact.
hackathon.focus: Agentic AI, Economic/Social Impact, Technological Innovation

sections.title: Agentic & Interactive AI
sections.description: Engineer autonomous agents that reason, plan, and use tools to solve complex challenges in education or industry, creating smart ecosystems

sections.title: Seamless User Experience
sections.description: Design and develop intuitive web/mobile interfaces with a focus on usability and accessibility.

sections.title: Scalable Cloud Architecture
sections.description: Implement cloud infrastructure to ensure your solution is robust and scalable.

sections.title: Measurable Impact
sections.description: Articulate the potential real-world impact and value proposition of your project.

checklist.text: Compete in a dedicated team of three innovators
Deliver a compelling final pitch to our expert jury panel

ctf.title: ENSET Challenge CTF: Cyber Showdown
ctf.description: Prepare for the ultimate cybersecurity trial! Following online qualifiers, top contenders will face off in a grueling 12-hour Capture The Flag final hosted live at ENSET Mohammedia.
ctf.focus: Web Application Security, Cryptography, Reverse Engineering, Network Forensics

sections.title: Web Exploitation
sections.description: Uncover and leverage critical vulnerabilities in web applications and services.

sections.title: Cryptographic Challenges
sections.description: Decipher encrypted messages, break codes, and master cryptographic techniques.

sections.title: Binary Analysis
sections.description: Reverse engineer software to understand functionality and find hidden flaws.

sections.title: Network & Forensics Investigation
sections.description: Analyze network traffic, investigate intrusions, and recover digital evidence.

checklist.text: Compete individually or form a cybersecurity team with 2-3 members
Battle through 12 hours of intense challenges on the ENSET campus

ctf.scoring: Points awarded based on challenge difficulty, speed of capture, and flag accuracy.

competition.eligibility: Participation is open to all students enrolled in any Moroccan university or higher education institution. For the CTF, participants may compete individually or form teams (recommended 2-3 members).

timeline.title: ENSET Challenge 2026 - Key Milestones
timeline.description: Navigate your path to victory! Follow these critical dates for the Hackathon and CTF.

steps.detail: Online registration opens for aspiring Hackathon teams. Gather your members and apply before the deadline.

phases.title: Phase 2: Virtual Proving Ground - Online Qualification

steps.detail: The online qualification round starts. Teams develop and submit their initial project concepts.
Our expert jury evaluates all submitted projects from the qualification round.
The top performing teams are selected and officially announced as finalists.

phases.title: Phase 3: The Main Event - On-Site Finals at ENSET

steps.detail: Finalists arrive for check-in, attend the opening ceremony, join workshops, and begin the 36-hour Hackathon.
Teams engage in intensive project development, attend technical workshops, and receive expert mentorship.
Teams finalize their projects, present their pitches to the judges at 09:00, and join the awards ceremony and networking event.
Online registration opens for aspiring Ctf teams. Gather your members and apply before the deadline.
CTF online qualification begins. Teams compete online to solve challenges during the 6-hour window.

phases.title: Phase 3: The Cyber Showdown - On-Site Final at ENSET

steps.detail: CTF finalists arrive at ENSET, complete check-in, and attend the main event activities.
The intense 12-hour on-site CTF final competition begins, testing a wide range of cybersecurity skills.
The CTF competition concludes. Final scores are determined, leading into the awards ceremony.

agenda.title: Finals Weekend Schedule
agenda.description: Key moments during the ENSET Challenge 2026 Grand Finals at ENSET Mohammedia (April 15-17).

items.detail: Intensive Development, Mentor Support & Tech Workshops

Final Pitches & Jury Deliberation ,

questions.q: Who is eligible to participate in ENSET Challenge 2026?
questions.a: The competition is open to all students currently enrolled in any recognized university, engineering school, or higher education institution within Morocco.

questions.q: What are the team size requirements?
questions.a: Hackathon participation requires teams of  three (3) students or two (2). For the CTF competition, participants may compete individually or form teams, with a recommended size of 2-4 members.

questions.q: Is there a fee to register or participate?
questions.a: No, participation in the ENSET Challenge 2026, including both the Hackathon and CTF finals, is entirely free of charge for all selected students.
For this Hackathon, proficiency in Agentic frameworks, LLM tool-use, full-stack development, and UI/UX design is advantageous for building autonomous ecosystems.

questions.q: What complimentary services are provided for finalists attending the ENSET Challenge 2026 event?
questions.a: Yes, finalists will receive full support during the event. Complimentary basic on-campus accommodation at ENSET Mohammedia will be arranged for those traveling from outside the greater Mohammedia/Casablanca metropolitan area. Additionally, all registered finalists will receive complimentary meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner) throughout the three-day final competition (April 15-17) hosted on the ENSET campus.
